title: Getting Started with Kotlin
content:
### Setting Up Kotlin

To start writing Kotlin code, you need the Kotlin compiler. The easiest way is to use IntelliJ IDEA, which includes Kotlin support out of the box.

---

### Installation Options

| Method | Command / Steps |
|--------|----------------|
| IntelliJ IDEA | Download from jetbrains.com, create new Kotlin project |
| SDKMAN | `sdk install kotlin` |
| Homebrew (macOS) | `brew install kotlin` |
| Manual | Download from GitHub releases |

---

### Your First Program

Create a file named `hello.kt` and run it with the Kotlin compiler.

> **Key Point:** The `fun` keyword declares a function. `main()` is the entry point of every Kotlin application.

---

### Running Kotlin Code

```bash
kotlinc hello.kt -include-runtime -d hello.jar
java -jar hello.jar
```

Or use the Kotlin REPL by typing `kotlinc` in the terminal.

> **Tip:** You can also use the Kotlin Playground at play.kotlinlang.org to run code online.

quiz.question: What keyword declares a function in Kotlin?
Which IDE provides the best Kotlin support out of the box?
